concerns.text: Task T010 (GEE Authentication) is placed AFTER T011a/T011 (Data Download). Authentication is a strict prerequisite for API access; T011 cannot execute without T010 completing first. This violates the 'Producer before consumer' rule where the auth token (artifact) is consumed by the download tasks.

Task T010 is marked [P] (parallel-safe). However, GEE authentication typically requires interactive login or a specific service account setup that must complete before any other task in the ingestion pipeline can run. It cannot safely run in parallel with T011a (Site Selection) or T011 (Download) as those tasks will fail immediately without the auth context established by T010.

Tasks T020 (Lagged Features) and T021 (GDD exclusion) are listed AFTER T014 (Interpolation) and T015/T016 (Exclusion/Flagging). Logically, feature engineering (T020/T021) that alters the input schema (removing GDD, creating lags) must occur BEFORE row-level filtering (T014/T015) to ensure the filtering logic operates on the final feature set. Currently, the order implies filtering raw data before feature transformation, which may lead to incorrect exclusion logic if the exclusion criteria depend on derived features.

Task T010 ID is duplicated. There is a test task 'T010 [P] [US1] Contract test...' and an implementation task 'T010 [P] [US1] Implement Google Earth Engine authentication...'. IDs must be unique to ensure deterministic ordering and traceability.
